text.title: LangChain and LangGraph: Building Production AI Agents — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `langchain` must enforce. Verifiable at review time; failures gate the artifact.

rule.statement: Use LCEL only when the chain is linear and stateless; use LangGraph the moment loops, retries, or human steps appear.
rule.rationale: LCEL is brittle once branching/state enter; rewriting mid-project costs more than starting on LangGraph.

rule.statement: Declare State as TypedDict with explicit reducer functions; never mutate state implicitly.
rule.rationale: Implicit mutation defeats checkpointing and replay.

rule.statement: Production graphs MUST configure a persistent checkpointer (PostgreSQL or Redis); MemorySaver is dev-only.
rule.rationale: Without checkpointer the graph cannot resume after crash or human approval.

rule.statement: Human-approval interrupts MUST fire BEFORE any irreversible mutation (DB write, payment, send-email).
rule.rationale: Interrupting after mutation cannot undo; interrupt-before is the only safe pattern.

rule.statement: Use Supervisor pattern when ≥3 independent agents collaborate; single-agent ReAct otherwise.
rule.rationale: Supervisor surfaces routing logic explicitly; ad-hoc multi-agent code becomes unauditable.

rule.statement: Every cyclic edge has a max-iteration cap; recursion_limit set at graph level.
rule.rationale: Unbounded loops are the #1 LangGraph production incident.
